mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future equity cash flows, directly boosting S&P 500 valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or forward returns are not significantly positive when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in real sales of existing homes and housing starts indicates a broad contraction in housing demand, which negatively impacts financial sector earnings and consumer confidence, driving S&P 500 returns lower over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns with a Rank IC > 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month drop in the index of industrial production below 98.0 reflects a severe supply-side shock that initially triggers risk-off sentiment, but in a post-shock recovery regime, it precedes aggressive monetary accommodation and positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are not significantly positive during post-shock recovery periods following an INDPRO drop below 98.0.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth rates reflect strengthening economic activity that supports corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or GDPC1 growth does not correlate with positive 60-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises by more than 0.5% month-over-month, it signals accelerating inflation that forces expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where nonfarm payrolls increase while the unemployment rate simultaneously rises indicates labor force participation swelling without corresponding job creation, signaling underlying labor market weakness that drags S&P 500 returns negative over the next month.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative returns with a Rank IC > 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1.5 million units, it marks a housing market downturn that typically coincides with broader credit tightening, but in a housing downturn regime, it triggers central bank liquidity injections that support positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are not significantly positive during housing downturn periods following HOUST drops below 1500.
